stuck_loop_check.reason: All 5 round-1 CRITICAL findings (DA-1..DA-5) adversarially re-checked and genuinely closed; no STOP-3 condition.

per_finding_verdicts.evidence: requirements.md:110 EARS header now says 'exactly the following 4 scenarios'; requirements.md:22 freezes RETIRE=4 with rationale; formal-criteria.md:14 (AC-T1-1-1) asserts 4 paths; formal-criteria.md:119 (AC-T1-12-1) asserts SKIP count >= 4; design.md:259,264 cardinality comments all say 4; traceability.md:130-133 enumerates exactly 4 RETIRE rows. Grep for any 'RETIRE=5' / '5 RETIRE' / '5 scenarios' in RETIRE context returned 0 active hits. Only 'synthesis 5' / 'double-count 5' appears as historical rationale in requirements.md:22 and the changelog entry at :643,645 — both explicitly framed as 'corrected DOWN from 5 to 4'. No contradiction.

REQ-T1-2 header at requirements.md:120 says 'exactly the following 16 scenarios'. Enumeration at requirements.md:122-139 lists 16 entries (verified by cross-checking traceability.md:36-149 where §A numbers them 5-20, including #19 pipeline-history-pii-scope and #20 pipeline-paused-webhook both tagged [RESTORED F2]). Scope-Freeze arithmetic at requirements.md:31 reads '13 KEEP + 16 REWRITE + 8 EXTEND + 3 v6.9.0-prefixed RETIRE = 40' and :36-47 V6100_TOUCHED set uses '16 files (REQ-T1-2)'. design.md:259 cardinality '16 REWRITE'. No '14 REWRITE' except inside historical/rationale prose at requirements.md:140 (source-of-truth note explaining correction up) and :643 (changelog initial-freeze entry) — both contextually clear. formal-criteria.md:24,27,31 (AC-T1-2-1/2) assert '16 REWRITE'. Orphans fully adopted.

Single formal definition V6100_TOUCHED := {...} at requirements.md:36-58. Every subsequent reference (REQ-T1-5 :165, REQ-T1-7 :182-184, AC-T1-5-1 :61-62, AC-T1-7-1/2 :73-78, design §4.2 :252-258) cites 'V6100_TOUCHED (formal definition in requirements.md Section 1 Scope Freeze)' — identical anchor text. No alternative scope wording (e.g., 'touched tests', 'new or rewritten', bare 'REWRITE + net-new') remains as a gate scope. Design.md:258 explicitly states 'V6100_TOUCHED enumeration — authoritative per requirements.md Section 1'. formal-criteria.md:61 declares 'SINGLE scope definition — identical to REQ-T1-7 AC-T1-7-2 and design §4.2'. No ambient ambiguity.

AC-T2-8-1 (formal-criteria.md:262-273) now grep-asserts 5 exact section headers: '^## Hook trigger conditions', '^## JSON input schema on stdin', '^## Exit code semantics', '^## Installation stanza example in ~/.claude/settings.json', '^## Confidence:'. Confidence check is hard grep '^## Confidence: HIGH' (per design.md §10). External citations asserted via regex '^## External citations' + 'https?://[^[:space:]]+' URL count >= 3 + 'retrieved:' date field YYYY-MM-DD. Not gameable by empty-paragraph-with-'HIGH'-stamp because 3 URLs + dated retrieval + 4 other mandatory sections gate the pass. MEDIUM/LOW routing documented (does NOT fail AC — activates REQ-T2-FALLBACK), separating outcome from schema-presence.

REQ-T2-3 (requirements.md:270+) adopts FILE SET approach. AC-T2-3-1 pins imperative-template count >= 37 via 'grep -rnF "Task(subagent_type=\'ceos-agents:" <5 exact files>'. AC-T2-3-2 pins permissive residual = 0 hard equality via 'grep -rnE \'(Run|Dispatch|Invoke) .*\(Task tool, model:\' <5 files>'. AC-T2-3-3 file-set existence check. Appendix A row 7 at requirements.md:632 restates the exact residual grep. Row 8 restates the imperative grep. Regex is specific enough — '(Run|Dispatch|Invoke)' + '\(Task tool, model:' won't overlap with the new-form imperative 'Task(subagent_type=...)'. Lower-bound '>=37' with '42 drift-5' rationale is the honest freeze.

new_adversarial_findings.summary: The changelog entry '2026-04-23 — initial freeze. User Gate 1 override applied (Track 1 REWRITE=14). ... RETIRE count corrected from synthesis's 5 to 4...' keeps the historical numbers '14' and '5' in prose. These are contextually safe because immediately followed by '- F1 (RETIRE=4)' and '- F2 (REWRITE=16)' bullets that supersede. Phase 5 fixer is unlikely to be misled, but a sloppy read could. Not a blocker — acceptable as revision audit trail. Recommend leaving as-is (removing it would erase the audit trail which is legitimately useful).
new_adversarial_findings.proposed_fix: Optional: rephrase to 'User Gate 1 override initially applied REWRITE=14 (synthesis prose); corrected to 16 per F2 (Phase 2 TABLE authority).' Cosmetic only.
new_adversarial_findings.phase_7_failure_mode: Negligible — audit prose, not normative scope clause.

new_adversarial_findings.summary: Track 1 effort revised 30h -> 33h (16 REWRITEs x 1.5h = 24h). Aggregate 42-48h -> 45-51h. Arithmetic is defensible: +2 REWRITEs x 1.5h = +3h, buffer +0h to aggregate lower bound (42+3=45) and +0h upper (48+3=51). Adequate. Judge-default effort concerns from DA-7 round 1 (19 net-new on 30h budget) now addressed — net-new table at design §8.4 enumerates 19 line items with REQ references, and Track 2 (~12h) + Track 3 (~3.5h) + Track 1 (~33h) = 48.5h midpoint within 45-51h range. Acceptable.

Verified: NO REQ IDs renumbered. REQ count stays 48. 2 ACs added (AC-T2-3-3, AC-T2-10-2), total 77 -> 79. All original REQ-T1-*, REQ-T2-*, REQ-T3-*, REQ-META-*, NFR-* identifiers preserved. AC-T2-8-1 re-worded in place (no ID shift). Clean.

Scanned for 'etc.' / 'and similar' / 'among others'. Two hits: (a) requirements.md:586 'The list is CLOSED — no "etc."' — explicit anti-pattern guard, correct usage. (b) design.md:401 comment '# AC-2: Marker pair presence (defensive — no bare END without START, etc.)' — inside a bash code-comment, not normative scope language. No open-ended scope creep.
